topic: extended sparkling wine lees ageing and texture
trigger_keywords: periodo largo de crianza sobre lias
crianza sobre lias en un vino espumoso

steps.label: cause
steps.text: A traditional-method sparkling wine spends a prolonged period in contact with the lees from its secondary fermentation.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: Autolysis releases mannoproteins and polysaccharides that increase palate weight and help stabilise the carbon dioxide bubbles, while other yeast compounds add aroma.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: The sparkling wine gains a creamier texture, finer and more persistent mousse, and greater complexity with yeast-derived notes.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: The sparkling wine gains a creamier texture, finer and more persistent mousse, and greater complexity with yeast-derived notes.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
